history.tip: Give her the headline first. Burying the point makes her hunt for what you actually mean.
Volunteer the receipt she didn't ask for. Transparency you initiate feels safer than transparency you surrender.
When he goes silent, he isn't punishing you — he's protecting himself.
Own your part first in every argument. Defense mode ends the moment accountability begins.
Ask 'what do you need from me right now?' before assuming she wants you to fix it.
Name the elephant she's pretending not to see. Unspoken tension erodes safety faster than hard conversations.
Praise her in public the way you critique her in private. Reputation shapes reality.
Kill the phone during her sentence. Divided attention tells her she's optional in her own home.
Say the thing you're editing in your head. The unsaid version becomes resentment by Friday.
50/50 marriages don't work. The math sounds fair. The math kills relationships.
Debrief the win, not just the wound. Couples who celebrate together survive the storms together.
Lead the calendar. If you don't plan the date, the marriage runs on leftovers.
